SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.repository.impl

Class DirtyCheckMetrics

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.repository.impl.DirtyCheckMetrics
All Implemented Interfaces:
DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
public final class DirtyCheckMetrics extends Object implements DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
Singleton JMX MBean for dirty checking metrics.

Registered as st.orm:type=DirtyCheckMetrics in the platform MBean server. All DirtySupport instances report to this single metrics collector. If JMX registration fails, metrics are still collected in-memory and accessible via getInstance().

Since:
1.9

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    long
    getCacheMisses()
    Number of checks where no cached baseline was available (entity not in cache or cache unavailable), causing a fallback to full-entity update.
    long
    getChecks()
    Total number of dirty checks performed.
    long
    getClean()
    Number of checks that found the entity clean (update skipped).
    long
    getCleanRatioPercent()
    Clean ratio as a percentage (0-100), indicating how often updates are avoided.
    long
    getDirty()
    Number of checks that found the entity dirty (update triggered).
    Map<String,String>
    getDirtyCheckPerEntity()
    Returns the effective DirtyCheck strategy per entity type.
    long
    getEntityModeChecks()
    Number of checks that used ENTITY update mode (full-row UPDATE on any change).
    long
    getEntityTypes()
    Number of distinct entity types that have generated UPDATE shapes.
    long
    getFieldClean()
    Number of individual field comparisons where the field was found equal (clean).
    long
    getFieldComparisons()
    Total number of individual field comparisons performed across all dirty checks.
    long
    getFieldDirty()
    Number of individual field comparisons where the field was found different (dirty).
    long
    getFieldModeChecks()
    Number of checks that used FIELD update mode (column-level UPDATE).
    long
    getIdentityMatches()
    Number of checks resolved by identity comparison (cached == entity), the cheapest path.
    static DirtyCheckMetrics
    getInstance()
    Returns the singleton metrics instance.
    long
    getInstanceStrategyChecks()
    Number of checks that used INSTANCE strategy (identity-based field comparison).
    Map<String,Integer>
    getMaxShapesPerEntity()
    Returns the configured maximum number of update shapes per entity type.
    long
    getShapes()
    Total number of distinct UPDATE statement shapes created across all entity types.
    Map<String,Long>
    getShapesPerEntity()
    Returns the number of distinct UPDATE shapes per entity type.
    Map<String,String>
    getUpdateModePerEntity()
    Returns the effective UpdateMode per entity type.
    long
    getValueStrategyChecks()
    Number of checks that used VALUE strategy (equality-based field comparison).

    Resets all counters to zero.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static DirtyCheckMetrics getInstance()
      Returns the singleton metrics instance.

    • registerEntity

      public void registerEntity(String entityType, String updateMode, String dirtyCheck, int maxShapes)
      Registers the effective dirty check configuration for the given entity type.
      Parameters:
      entityType - the simple class name of the entity type.
      updateMode - the effective update mode name.
      dirtyCheck - the effective dirty check strategy name.
      maxShapes - the configured maximum number of update shapes.

    • recordClean

      public void recordClean()

    • getShapes

      public long getShapes()

      Total number of distinct UPDATE statement shapes created across all entity types.

      getShapes in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean

    • getShapesPerEntity

      public Map<String,Long> getShapesPerEntity()
      Description copied from interface: DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
      Returns the number of distinct UPDATE shapes per entity type.

      Each key is the simple class name of the entity type, and the value is the number of distinct column combinations (shapes) that have been generated for that type. Compare with the configured storm.update.max_shapes to gauge overflow pressure per entity type.

      Specified by:
      getShapesPerEntity in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean

    • getUpdateModePerEntity

      public Map<String,String> getUpdateModePerEntity()
      Description copied from interface: DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
      Returns the effective UpdateMode per entity type.

      Each key is the simple class name of the entity type, and the value is the update mode name (e.g., "FIELD", "ENTITY").

      Specified by:
      getUpdateModePerEntity in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean

    • getDirtyCheckPerEntity

      public Map<String,String> getDirtyCheckPerEntity()
      Description copied from interface: DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
      Returns the effective DirtyCheck strategy per entity type.

      Each key is the simple class name of the entity type, and the value is the dirty check strategy name (e.g., "INSTANCE", "VALUE").

      Specified by:
      getDirtyCheckPerEntity in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean

    • getMaxShapesPerEntity

      public Map<String,Integer> getMaxShapesPerEntity()
      Description copied from interface: DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
      Returns the configured maximum number of update shapes per entity type.

      Each key is the simple class name of the entity type, and the value is the max shapes limit.

      Specified by:
      getMaxShapesPerEntity in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Description copied from interface: DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
      Resets all counters to zero.

      reset in interface DirtyCheckMetricsMXBean
